signals.headline: South Korea** updates show Bank of Korea (BoK) holding steady after ending rate-cut cycle, with March CPI at 2
signals.raw: South Korea** updates show Bank of Korea (BoK) holding steady after ending rate-cut cycle, with March CPI at 2.2% due to government measures, but inflation projected near 3% by May/June amid energy risks and weak won at 1,457/USD; BoK monitors Fed moves to avoid capital outflows.[2][3] Global forecasts predict stable core CPI at 2.8% in 2026, with U.S. at 3.2% and BoK on prolonged hold near 2%.[4]
